# go-jsonrpc

> `go-jsonrpc` is a transport-neutral JSON-RPC 2.0 server and client package for Go. It is designed for production APIs: protocol behavior is explicit, errors are auditable, middleware is composable, HTTP is optional, and malformed input is covered by conformance and fuzz tests.

This index is generated from the repository's canonical Markdown documentation.

## Documentation

- [Quickstart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/quickstart.md): Register transport-independent handlers, build a dispatcher, then choose an adapter. A handler receives the request context and raw params so it can choose positional, named, custom, or typed decoding.
- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/architecture.md): The package separates the protocol from every delivery mechanism. The normal server flow is:
- [Public API reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/api.md): This is the semantic reference for the exported surface. Exact Go signatures are also available through `go doc github.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc`.
- [Middleware, authentication, and observability](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/middleware.md): Dispatcher middleware wraps method execution without changing the transport. It is appropriate for RPC-aware authentication, authorization, logging, tracing, metrics, deadlines, and correlation. Ordinary `net/http` middleware remains the right place for TLS identity, HTTP headers, CORS, or IP controls.
- [Scenario cookbook](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/cookbook.md): `DecodeParams` rejects unknown fields. Use `json.Unmarshal` directly when an API has a deliberate forward-compatible or partially dynamic params object.
- [Production adoption guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/adoption.md): Adopt the package at a protocol boundary, not by rewriting business logic at the same time. The sequence below keeps wire compatibility observable and rollback straightforward.
- [Troubleshooting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/troubleshooting.md): Start by separating HTTP transport failures from JSON-RPC error objects. HTTP statuses other than 200 or 204 indicate the adapter or upstream rejected the exchange. A 200 response may contain either `result` or `error`.
- [FAQ](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/faq.md): No. `Dispatcher` and `Client` depend only on bytes, context, and the `Transport` interface. The package includes HTTP adapters because they are common and easy to compose with `net/http`.
- [Compatibility policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/compatibility.md): The project follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/) for tagged releases. Protocol behavior is compatibility-sensitive even where a Go type or function signature does not change.
- [JSON-RPC 2.0 conformance matrix](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/conformance.md): This matrix maps every normative JSON-RPC 2.0 rule to implementation, test, and documentation evidence. The official [JSON-RPC 2.0 specification](https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification) is authoritative. Defensive JSON policies are labeled separately and do not claim to be additional JSON-RPC requirements.
- [Hardening report](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/hardening.md): This report records the evidence-driven security, correctness, interoperability, and operational audit of `go-jsonrpc`. It is a living audit artifact: an `Open` disposition is a release blocker at the stated severity, not an implied acceptance of the risk.
- [Versioning and release guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/docs/releasing.md): Releases are immutable semantic-version tags created from a clean, reviewed `main` commit. The GitHub tag workflow reruns tests, race detection, coverage, and vet before creating release notes.
- [Roadmap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/ROADMAP.md): The roadmap is ordered by protocol confidence, not feature count. New adapters must reuse the same envelopes, validation, dispatch, and error semantics.
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/CHANGELOG.md): All notable changes to this project are documented here. The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and the project uses [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).

## Complete documentation

- [llms-full.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-jsonrpc/main/llms-full.txt): All linked documentation in one file.
